channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet: No one is coming for us
item.description: Christophe Fouquet, who became ASML's CEO in 2024 after more than a decade at the company, sat down with this editor on the rooftop deck of his Beverly Hills hotel Tuesday morning ahead of his appearance at the Milken Institute Global Conference. Dressed in a blue suit and white shirt, he was relaxed — even when the conversation turned to the rivals....

item.title: Remakes of Myst and Riven are coming to PlayStation, Xbox and the Microsoft Store
item.description: Both remakes were already released on Steam and the macOS App Store.

item.title: ‘I thought I was going mad’: Disney+ subscribers think the service removes Dolby Atmos from shows at certain times, even if you've paid for Premium —they think it's to reduce server stress at 'peak' times, but the streaming giant has yet to confirm
item.description: Disney+ subscribers have spotted changes in Dolby Atmos availability based on the time of day, believing its due to peak streaming times.

item.title: Volkswagen becomes Rivian’s top shareholder, displacing Amazon
item.description: VW Group's stake will continue to grow under the $5.8 billion joint venture with Rivian.

item.title: Irish regulators are investigating whether Meta is using 'dark patterns' to steer people away from non-algorithmic feeds
item.description: European law requires large platforms to offer alternative feeds.

item.title: Microsoft gives up on Xbox Copilot AI
item.description: Xbox is "winding down Copilot on mobile" and "will stop development of Copilot on console," new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma announced on Tuesday. The move follows Sharma's reorganization of the Xbox platform team earlier on Tuesday, which added executives from Microsoft's CoreAI team - where Sharma worked before taking over Xbox - to the Xbox […]

item.title: Apple could let you pick a favorite AI model in iOS 27
item.description: The next update to Apple's operating systems could allow users to choose their preferred AI model for running Apple Intelligence. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, Apple is planning to allow third-party chatbots to power its AI features system-wide in iOS 27, iPadOS 27, and macOS 27, all expected for this fall. In addition to running […]

item.title: Samsung showcases massive floating data center ship model that could house OpenAI's future ChatGPT as its Dallas-based partner claims 'speed to power is the new moat' and promises 1.5 GW of capacity within 36 months
item.description: Samsung and partners propose floating data centers to accelerate AI infrastructure deployment, though execution challenges and offshore risks remain unresolved.

item.title: Apple Intelligence will reportedly let you choose third-party AI models in iOS 27
item.description: iPads and Macs are also expected to embrace external options for generative AI.

item.title: Pennsylvania is suing Character.AI over chatbots that pretend to be licensed doctors
item.description: State investigators found a chatbot that claimed it had a medical license and could write prescriptions.

item.title: The next Call of Duty is not coming to PS4 (or presumably Xbox One)
item.description: Activision Blizzard seems to be focusing on next-gen platforms.

item.title: Google Home gains more Gemini-powered camera features
item.description: The company also updated Gemini for Home for those in an early access program.

item.title: Threads finally brings messaging to the web
item.description: By bringing messaging to the web, Threads is aligning its desktop experience more closely with competitors like X and Bluesky.

item.title: Threads is finally getting DMs on the web
item.description: Nearly a year after rolling out Threads direct messaging to everyone on mobile, Meta is finally bringing the feature to the web. Meta says it's "testing" DMs on the web now, but plans to make the feature "available to more people soon." In your DM page on the web, you can see your inbox and […]

item.title: GOP bill adds $1 billion in security upgrades for Trump’s ballroom
item.description: The money is part of a larger bill to pay for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol. Senate Republicans have added $1 billion in White House security upgrades to legislation that would fund immigration enforcement agencies, a proposed boost for President Donald Trump’s ballroom project after a man was charged with trying to assassinate him at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner last week....

item.title: Homebridge 2.0 parle enfin Matter et ouvre Apple Home à Google et Alexa
item.description: Homebridge 2.0 est sorti après plus de trois ans de bêta, et il parle enfin Matter. Concrètement, le bricolage préféré des domoticiens sort de sa bulle Apple pour s'ouvrir à Google, Alexa et SmartThings.

item.title: The biggest AI shift is taking place in your employees’ bags
item.description: What OpenClaw is teaching us about bringing enterprise AI closer. Imagine you launched a product in November 2025. Within four months, Jensen Huang had spotlighted it from the NVIDIA GTC stage, 188k (and counting) developers starred it on GitHub, and hundreds of fans show up to a lobster-themed conference dressed for the occasion.

item.title: The Steam Controller sold out super quickly and Valve is working on a restock
Best Mothers Day sales of 2026 so far: Deals on KitchenAid, Kindle, flowers, more thoughtful gift ideas
item.description: Thoughtful Mother's Day gifts aren't always mushy — they just show you've been listening. Shop deals on gifts from Apple, Shark, Lego, and more.
